meaning: n.边缘,须边,刘海adj.边缘的,额外的v.在...上加以缘饰,成为...的边缘
examples: My fringe was cut short, but the rest of my hair was long.
The monarchists are a small fringe group who quarrel fiercely among themselves.
They are paid their salaries and are entitled to fringe benefits on a pro-rata basis.
Situated on the western fringe of Greece's Dodecanese islands, Karpathos itself feels rather remote.
Despite its history, wrestling somehow lost out to several fringe events, such as modern pentathlon.
